rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ELERWEASE BRADFORD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES CONSERVATORSHIP (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Conservator is requesting the following: a. $31,660.47 in reimbursements. No receipt available for 11/6/2019 entry for $428.45. Conservator alleges for SFPUC water/sewer. Whether the items not included in prior account but covered by the prior account period should be allowed and included as part of the request for reimbursement. i. 10/01/2016 Lucky $224.90 ii. 7/21/2016 SFDPH for $334.78. iii. 10/12/16 for $78.00 certified Letters Mathis iv. 5/31/2016 for Airport Appliance $270.73. b. Attorney fees of $24,510.00 and costs of $511.72. i. Court to determine entry 4/4/18 letters from Navia, PC w/ client, Drafting objections for both cases for 2.5 hours or $1,000.00. c. The court order Daniel Conrad to repay interest in the sum of $2,356.62 within 30 days from the date of its order. Petitioner attached Examiner Notes, a brief, and an order from an unrelated unreported case which does not appear to be valid legal authority. Daniel A. Conrad filed objections. d. Bond should be reduced to $10,000.00 and future interim accounts be waived. 2) Conservator incurred $210.00 in overdraft charges. 3) Conservator misused the debt card causing $252.24 in charges. $90.86 has been refunded to the conservatorship. $161.38 yet to be repaid.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD GORDON SELF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing with Judge's approval, but specifically DISAPPROVING the administrator's distributions made without a Court order and his overpayment of compensation to himself. The second amended petition still fails to correct the erroneous date of the administrator's appointment and the date of the order authorizing the preliminary distribution, indicates that he is the administrator of the "will", still shows the incorrect total of the I&As in the calculation of the fee base, states that of $256,435.90 was the difference between the unauthorized preliminary distributions totaling $450,458 and the authorized preliminary distribution totaling $186,197, does not indicate to whom the decedent's truck was "given away", merely refers to a prior petition instead of including the disclosures so that this second amended petition was complete in itself, erroneously still refers to the statutory time for filing a claim against a decedent rather than against an estate after Letters are issued to a personal representative [Probate Code Sections 9100, 9150(c) and (d), and 9253], alleges that $241,525.19 + $74,478.85 + $74,478,85 = $372,394.26, claims that 1/3 of $482,852.92 (the purported total amount for distribution) is $160,338.59 and that $160,338.50 less the $171,743 preliminary distribution the administrator made to himself is $11,417.75, does not explain why only additional property exceeding $7,161.30, instead of $6,848.44, is to be distributed equally to the three heirs, and shows yet different amounts in the prayer at paragraph 9. An additional $235 in filing fee will be ordered to be paid.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ADAM AND EMELIA ROMERO REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1)Petitioner request this Court to make the following findings: a.Emelia, Joseph, and Marina have knowingly and voluntarily entered into the settlement agreement. b. The agreement settles and resolves all known and unknown claims that exist or may exist between Emelia and Joseph (except for any landlord-tenant claims). 2) The Court to determine whether the following terms of the settlement agreement should be approved: a. Paragraph 19: " all attorney's fees incurred by Emelia through the effective date are properly charged to the Trust." b. Paragraph 34: "Emelia's communications with her counsel shall be personal to Emelia, who shall have no obligation and may not be compelled by court order to turn over or disclose any privileged communications to any successor trustee." 3) The "4th Amendment" was not a part of the original petition, which includes a new distribution to Isabella Romero (a minor), in trust. Neither she nor Adam Romero, Jr. are parties to the settlement agreement. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: SARA R. JULIAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF ISAAC JULIAN BOOTS,DATED 1/16/18 EST. PURSUANT TO COURT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/4/2021. 1) Please explain why counsel has not complied with the local rules regarding the ex parte petition to increase bond. An account will not be approved until the additional bond is on file. 2) Please file a complete copy of the trust documents filed 01/29/2019. There appear to be missing pages. 3) Please provide facts in support of the petitioner's request for fees on account as required under Probate Code section 2643(c). 4) Please explain why no inventory and appraisal has been filed. 5) Please explain and provide legal authority for the distribution to the Trust made in installments of $25,000.00 on 10/10/19 and 441,182.01 on 02/24/2020 and for not totaling the amount ordered of $500,000.00. The 12/20/2018 order appears to have authorized a 1 time transfer of $500,000.00. 6) Please explain and provide legal authority for the child support payments. 7) Petitioner must verify the assets with Morgan Stanley. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 8) On the trustee fees please explain why there appears to be two duplicate entries for 01/31/2019 Made deposits, transfers, set up accounts for $36.25. 9) On trustee fees please explain the entries for bill payments from 02/14/2019 to 06/06/2019 when there are no such disbursements in the account for this time period. 10) On trustee fees please explain entry 6/27/2019 reconciled client accounts for 2.5 hours. 11) On trustee fees please explain entry 11/4/2019 bookkeeper posted to clients accounts for $50.75. 12) On trustee fees please explain what is the nature and need of "updated client records" occurring on a frequent basis? 13) On trustee fees please explain entry 12/23/2019 staff prepared monthly service reports for $18.85. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHIKO KAWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/02/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) Paragraph 6 of the petition must also allege whether any heir HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined within the meaning of Probate Code section 9202(b). 2) A proof that the estate funds exceeding the FDIC limit have been segregated and held in insured accounts at additional institutions must be filed. 3) Original bank statements verifying the ending balances must be filed. If e-filed, a declaration re the retention of the originals must be filed. See LRSF 14.60(D). 4) Exhibit E must comply with Probate Code sections 1061, 1062, and 1063. 5) Was notice of proposed action given before disposing of the tangible personal property inventoried as an asset of this estate? See Probate Code section 10513. 6) An updated schedule of proposed distribution must be filed in light of the Court's recent order determining entitlement of the predeceased spouse's children to distribution. An updated proposed order should also be submitted.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MABEL A. ROBINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/28/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBINA DEMARTINI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither the mailed nor published notice shows that a CORRECTED petition was filed. The Judicial Council only recognizes an amendment to a petition, a fully amended petition, or a supplement to a petition. [CRC 7.3.] The Court to determine if notice should be waived for the "Corrected" petition. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED BY DECLARATION OF TRUST DATED MAY 16, 2012, BY DAVID J. MACKIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Confirm Trust Ownership Of Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] This does not appear to be the proper venue as one of the petitioning co-trustees appears to reside in Rolling Hills Estates, California, not San Francisco, and the other appears to reside in Oregon. If the service list attached to the Memorandum of Points and Authorities is more recent and accurate, then neither co-trustee appears to reside in California. [Probate Code Sections 17002 and 17005.] The incorrect form of Notice of Hearing was served in any event. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner before 7/23/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDVIN PETER OLSEN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 07/01/2022. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 07/18/2022.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AMY MARIETTA LOVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/21/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who was the parent or who were the parents of the nieces and nephew and when did that sibling or those siblings die? Who was the predeceased spouse and when did he die? A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address these issues. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/12/21. (Bond will be for the full estimated value of the estate, not reduced by the petitioner's estimated encumbrance.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WING H. LOUIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/2021 1) Please file all required documents separately. 2) Duties and Liabilities must be filed separately. 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Please file proof of publication. 5) Please file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 6) Please file declarations signed by the named executors under penalty of perjury declining to serve. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order for probate (DE-140) to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEUNG KOOT TSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 J.] The proof of service is incompletely verified (not dated). Item 3a of the petition is incomplete. Item 3d(7) of the petition is incomplete. Items 5a(3) or (4), 5b, and 7 of the petition must be answered. Who was the decedent's father, and when did he die? No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed yet. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. The information missing from the petition must be set forth in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/16/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
